name: Russia
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **RUSSIA URANIUM-CUSTODIAN TRACK OFFICIALLY SIDELINED — KREMLIN PESKOV CLARIFIED MAY 5: OFFER 'NOT CURRENTLY ON THE NEGOTIATING TABLE.'** Day 9 of 21 of the Russia-mediator window (closes 2026-05-11) is now structurally answered NO — Pakistan track is primary; Trump's May 5 pause framework cited 'Pakistan's request' explicitly, not Russia. Lavrov reaffirmed the offer to host Iran's enriched uranium in early May — but Peskov's May 5 clarification puts the offer on indefinite hold. Iran routed the 14-point proposal via Pakistan (not Russia); the Pakistan-Saudi security architecture has displaced Russia from the operational mediator role. Russia retains structural facets: rhetorical support for Iran via state media, intelligence support via permanent communications channel, UNSC veto, Bushehr two new nuclear units commitment. Russian-oil waiver (30-day India extension) preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners. The Apr 27 Putin-Araghchi Kremlin meeting produced reaffirmation of standing offers but no architecture-advance; the Russia-custodian 21-day window is now resolved-NO at Day 9. TASS reported the Kremlin uranium-offer reaffirmation factually without escalation — characteristic of Moscow accepting the sidelined position.

active_triggers.condition: Russia announces formal proposal as nuclear-material custodian within 14 days of Putin-Araghchi meeting (by 2026-05-11)

recent_signals.headline: RUSSIA NUCLEAR-CUSTODIAN TRACK STRUCTURALLY SIDELINED BY PAKISTAN-ROUTE PROPOSAL
PUTIN-ARAGHCHI APR 27 MEETING REAFFIRMED APR 13 STANDING OFFER, DID NOT TABLE NEW FORMAL PROPOSAL — DAY 1 OF 14-DAY CUST

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. Russia providing intelligence to Iran including data on US military positions

10 signals. Russia remains an intelligence/diplomatic supporter of Iran | Russia friendly-flag in Iran's three-tier Hormuz system per Bloomberg Tracker — vessels flagged to Russia continue to transit | Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UNSC resolution to keep Hormuz open (April 7, 11-2 vote, France abstained)
